behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the target file and describes all five issues (headless mode, executablePath, chmod permissions, infinite timeouts, retry delay) that tests verify.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes problems (e.g., 'headless configuration is needed for macOS', 'retry delay may be insufficient') without specifying 'shell' mode, 3000ms, or exact timeout values.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_macos_headless_shell_mode hardcodes the string 'shell' as the required headless value, but the instruction only says 'a different headless configuration is needed' — alternative valid fixes like headless: 'new' or custom configuration would fail. Also test_retry_delay_increased requires >1000ms but gold uses 3000 — acceptable threshold, but the shell string is a specific gold-only literal.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests extract expressions and evaluate them via _run_node subprocess, and test_chmod_cached_binaries actually creates files and verifies execute bits after running chmod commands.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each test requires real behavioral changes — a stub wouldn't produce 'shell' on darwin, wouldn't exclude executablePath, wouldn't make files executable, etc.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five issues listed in instruction.md have corresponding tests (headless shell mode, executablePath exclusion, chmod, finite timeouts, retry delay).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names, docstrings, and section comment headers like '# Fail-to-pass (pr_diff) — core behavioral fixes'.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code fix, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and identifiers appear correct and consistent.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file but localization isn't the challenge; exact lines/fixes aren't disclosed.
